---
title: "Journal Entry 1: Research Idea"
date: "2026-01-15"
categories: [research-idea, planning]
description: "What is your research idea?"
---

![Research planning](../AI_Error.jpeg)

## What is your research idea?

I want to investigate the problem of getting errors when using LLMs and coding agents. There have been many times when I’ve gotten frustrated over errors popping up when using a coding agent. For example, it tries to reference a method that doesn’t exist. My idea for solving this problem is to take the LLM output and mutate it to purposefully induce errors. This output will then be given to the coding agent, and if an error is present, you will then know what mutation caused the error, allowing you to adjust the coding agent to account for the possible error. I hope to create a solution that will catch possible errors as a preventive measure rather than fixing the problem only after getting an error.

A paper related to LLM agents failing is Kunlun Zhu, et al. "Where LLM Agents Fail and How They Can Learn From Failures." (2025) https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.25370
